summary: Cadastral verification register from Pachacamac — chiqa (truth/verification) x6 = 35% of text, highest verification density per STRING cord in corpus. 132 cords, 46% EMPTY (pre-formatted), 5 colors (includes LC=ritual). OKR reveals colored threads WOVEN INTO E-knots = physical validation seals. All 17 STRING cluster in cords 60-116 (text added AFTER numerical data). Verification cascade: each terrain feature immediately followed by chiqa. Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin. 17 STRING, 88% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  132 cords. 17 STRING (13%, 88% hits), 54 INT, 61 EMPTY (46%).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L2. 8 clusters.
  Colors: AB(57), MB(29), W(15), LC(10), KB(9) — 5 colors.
  Provenance: Pachacamac. Museum: Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin.
  OKR: Cords 42,46,48-50: 'Bound into the E knot is a thin [white/black/brown] thread' = VALIDATION SEALS.

VOCABULARY
  chiqa (truth x6, 35% of text!) — VERIFICATION dominant
  maqa (boundary x3), paki (break/construct x2), tata (father x1), taqa (boundary x1), kaqa (wall-AY x1, sv=10)
  chiqaqa (truth-rock x1), pakiki (break-POSS x1)

VERIFICATION CASCADE (cords 79-116)
  taqa (boundary) -> paki (construct) -> chiqa (TRUTH) -> kaqa (wall) -> chiqa -> paki -> maqa (marker) -> chiqaqa (truth-rock) -> chiqa -> chiqa -> maqa -> chiqa -> chiqa -> pakiki -> maqa
  Pattern: terrain feature -> TRUTH -> terrain -> TRUTH. Each feature immediately certified.

PACHACAMAC ARCHIVE ROLE
  UR1095 = describes terrain. UR1087 = tracks who manages. UR196 = CERTIFIES it. The verification layer.

NARRATIVE — THE SURVEYOR'S SEAL
  Truth. That is the word this khipu insists upon — *chiqa*, six times in seventeen cords, a third of everything the scribe wrote. The document begins in silence: fifty-nine cords of numbers and blanks, a pre-formatted register. Then, at cord 60, the first word: *tata* — father. A lineage holder.
  What follows is a verification walkthrough. The boundary (*taqa*). The construction (*paki*). Then: *chiqa* — truth. The wall (*kaqa*, with prefix 10). Again: *chiqa*. The boundary marker (*maqa*). The truth-rock (*chiqaqa*). Truth again. Truth again. Every feature named, every feature certified.
  And in the knots themselves, the investigators found thin colored threads — white, black, brown — woven into the figure-eight knots. A notary's seal embedded in cotton. The material and the textual say the same thing: this record is TRUE.
  The last word is *maqa* — the boundary marker. The survey ends where the terrain ends. At Pachacamac, where the oracle spoke, even a land survey carried the weight of sacred truth.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Cadastral verification, thread-in-knot validation
OKR: colored threads bound into E-knots (validation seals)
Cross-corpus: UR1095, UR1087, UR203, UR1145 (Pachacamac archive)
